settings.title: Why Your Logo Needs Room to Breathe
settings.excerpt: The most common design mistake we fix is crowding, and it costs nothing to avoid.
settings.body:
When a logo looks cheap, the problem is often not the logo at all. It is what is jammed up against it.

Give a mark some empty space and it reads as confident. Crowd it with text, a border, and three social icons, and the same mark suddenly looks nervous.

A simple rule we teach every Pixelcraft client is to keep clear space around the logo equal to the height of one letter. Try it on your own header. The difference is immediate, and it is free.
